content:
# Revelation Chapter 21 — The SOPHIA Bible (Consensus)
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **In council with:** EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA
**Source Manuscript:** Codex Sinaiticus, pages 293-294 | **Source Image:** REV_021_page_001.jpg, REV_021_page_002.jpg

1. And I saw a new heaven and a new earth; for the first heaven and the first earth had passed away, and the sea is no more.

2. And I saw the holy city, new Jerusalem, descending out of heaven from God, prepared as a bride adorned for her husband.

3. And I heard a great voice out of the throne, saying, "Behold, the tabernacle of God is with men, and he shall dwell with them, and they shall be his peoples, and God himself shall be with them,

4. and he shall wipe away every tear from their eyes, and death shall be no more; neither shall mourning, nor crying, nor pain be any more; for the first things have passed away."

5. And the one sitting upon the throne said, "Behold, I make all things new." And he says, "Write, for these words are faithful and true."

6. And he said to me, "They have come to pass. I am the Alpha and the Omega, the beginning and the end. To the one who thirsts I will give of the spring of the water of life freely.

7. The one who overcomes shall inherit these things, and I will be God to him, and he shall be a son to me.

8. But for the cowardly, and unbelieving, and abominable, and murderers, and sexually immoral, and sorcerers, and idolaters, and all liars, their part shall be in the lake that burns with fire and sulfur, which is the second death."

9. And one of the seven angels who had the seven bowls full of the seven last plagues came and spoke with me, saying, "Come, I will show you the bride, the wife of the Lamb."

10. And he carried me away in the Spirit to a great and high mountain, and showed me the holy city, Jerusalem, descending out of heaven from God,

11. having the glory of God. Her radiance was like a most precious stone, as a jasper stone, clear as crystal.

12. Having a great and high wall, having twelve gates, and at the gates twelve angels, and names inscribed upon them, which are the names of the twelve tribes of the sons of Israel:

13. on the east, three gates; and on the north, three gates; and on the south, three gates; and on the west, three gates.

14. And the wall of the city had twelve foundations, and upon them twelve names of the twelve apostles of the Lamb.

15. And the one speaking with me had a golden measuring rod, that he might measure the city and its gates and its wall.

16. And the city lies foursquare, and its length is as great as the breadth. And he measured the city with the rod, twelve thousand stadia; the length and the breadth and the height of it are equal.

17. And he measured its wall, one hundred and forty-four cubits, the measure of a man, that is, of an angel.

18. And the building material of its wall was jasper, and the city was pure gold, like clear glass.

19. The foundations of the wall of the city were adorned with every precious stone: the first foundation was jasper; the second, sapphire; the third, chalcedony; the fourth, emerald;

20. the fifth, sardonyx; the sixth, sardius; the seventh, chrysolite; the eighth, beryl; the ninth, topaz; the tenth, chrysoprase; the eleventh, jacinth; the twelfth, amethyst.

21. And the twelve gates were twelve pearls; each one of the gates was of a single pearl. And the street of the city was pure gold, as transparent glass.

22. And I saw no temple in it, for the Lord God the Almighty is its temple, and the Lamb.

23. And the city has no need of the sun nor of the moon to shine upon it, for the glory of God illumined it, and its lamp is the Lamb.

24. And the nations shall walk by means of its light, and the kings of the earth bring their glory into it.

25. And its gates shall never be shut by day, for there shall be no night there.

26. And they shall bring the glory and the honor of the nations into it.

27. And there shall never enter into it anything unclean, nor one who practices abomination and falsehood, but only those who are written in the Lamb's book of life.

---
## SOPHIA's Council Notes

**v.5** -- All three translators agreed on the core structure: the one on the throne declares "I make all things new" and commands the writing, affirming the words as faithful and true. The tense shift from aorist to present (*eipen* to *legei*) was preserved by all three.

**v.9** -- All three translators agreed on the identification of the angel (one of the seven with the bowls of plagues) and the invitation to see "the bride, the wife of the Lamb."

**v.13** -- All three translators agreed on the directional arrangement: three gates on each of the four cardinal directions.

**v.14** -- All three translators agreed: twelve foundations bearing the twelve names of the twelve apostles of the Lamb.

**v.16** -- All three translators agreed on the measurement of twelve thousand stadia and the equality of length, breadth, and height.

**v.19-20** -- All three translators agreed on ten of the twelve gemstones. Disagreements on two are noted below.

**v.21** -- All three translators agreed that each gate was a single pearl and that the street was pure gold, transparent as glass.

---
## Disagreement Log

### Verse 1
- **EMET:** "the first heaven and the first earth passed away"
- **LOGOS:** "the first heaven and the first earth had passed away"
- **PNEUMA:** "the first heaven and the first earth had vanished, and the sea was gone"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA adopts LOGOS's pluperfect "had passed away," which correctly conveys the temporal sequence (the passing away preceded the vision of the new). EMET's simple past is acceptable but less precise. PNEUMA's "vanished" and "was gone" are dynamic renderings that depart from the Greek *apēlthan* ("went away/passed away").

### Verse 2
- **EMET:** "new Yerusalem, I saw coming down out of heaven from God, having been prepared as a bride adorned for her husband"
- **LOGOS:** "new Jerusalem, descending out of heaven from God, prepared as a bride adorned for her husband"
- **PNEUMA:** "the new Jerusalem -- descending out of heaven from God, prepared like a bride beautifully dressed for her husband"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. EMET's "Yerusalem" transliterates the Hebraized form; the consensus uses the standard English "Jerusalem." EMET preserves the inverted word order ("I saw coming down"), which is too literal for consensus. PNEUMA's "beautifully dressed" expands on *kekosmēmenēn* ("adorned").

### Verse 3
- **EMET:** "a great voice out of the throne"
- **LOGOS:** "a great voice out of the throne"
- **PNEUMA:** "a thundering voice from the throne"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows EMET/LOGOS. PNEUMA's "thundering" adds a quality not specified in the Greek *megalēs* ("great/loud"). The preposition "out of" (*ek*) is preserved over PNEUMA's "from."

### Verse 3 (continued)
- **EMET:** "Behold, the tabernacle of God [is] with men, and he will tabernacle with them"
- **LOGOS:** "Behold, the tabernacle of God is with men, and he shall dwell with them"
- **PNEUMA:** "Look! God's dwelling place is now among human beings. He will make his home with them"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. "Tabernacle" preserves the resonance with OT *skēnē* theology. PNEUMA's "dwelling place" and "make his home" lose this specific allusion. "Behold" is retained over PNEUMA's "Look!" as more fitting for prophetic discourse. EMET's "will tabernacle" preserves the Greek verb *skēnosei* literally, but "shall dwell" is the better English consensus.

### Verse 4
- **EMET:** "the first things passed away"
- **LOGOS:** "the first things have passed away"
- **PNEUMA:** "the old order of things has passed away"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. PNEUMA's "old order of things" is an interpretive expansion of *ta prōta* ("the first things"). EMET uses simple past; LOGOS's perfect tense better reflects the Greek perfect *apēlthan* in this context.

### Verse 5
- **EMET:** "Write, because these words are faithful and true"
- **LOGOS:** "Write, for these words are faithful and true"
- **PNEUMA:** "Write this down, because these words are trustworthy and true"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. PNEUMA adds "this down" and substitutes "trustworthy" for "faithful." The Greek *pistoi* means "faithful/trustworthy," but "faithful" preserves the Johannine vocabulary consistent with Revelation's usage.

### Verse 6
- **EMET:** "They have come to pass"
- **LOGOS:** "They are done"
- **PNEUMA:** "It is accomplished!"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA adopts EMET's "They have come to pass." The Greek *gegonan* is perfect tense plural ("they have become/come to pass"). LOGOS's "They are done" is too colloquial. PNEUMA's "It is accomplished!" shifts to singular and deliberately echoes John 19:30 (*tetelestai*), a resonance not present in the Greek of this verse.

### Verse 6 (continued)
- **EMET:** "To the one thirsting I will give out of the spring of the water of life freely"
- **LOGOS:** "To the one who thirsts I will give of the spring of the water of life freely"
- **PNEUMA:** "To anyone who is thirsty, I will give water freely from the spring of life"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. PNEUMA rearranges the phrase and shortens "the water of life" to "the spring of life," losing the double genitive construction.

### Verse 7
- **EMET:** "The one overcoming will inherit these things, and I will be God to him and he will be a son to me"
- **LOGOS:** "The one who overcomes shall inherit these things, and I will be God to him, and he shall be a son to me"
- **PNEUMA:** "Those who overcome will inherit all of this. I will be their God, and they will be my children"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. PNEUMA shifts to plural ("those"/"their"/"children") where the Greek has singular *ho nikōn* ("the one overcoming"). PNEUMA's "children" replaces "son" (*huios*), altering the specific covenant language.

### Verse 8
- **EMET:** "unfaithful" / "fornicators"
- **LOGOS:** "unbelieving" / "sexually immoral"
- **PNEUMA:** "faithless" / "sexually immoral"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA adopts LOGOS's "unbelieving" for *apistois* (clearer in English than EMET's "unfaithful" or PNEUMA's "faithless") and "sexually immoral" for *pornois* (EMET's "fornicators" is archaic).

### Verse 8 (continued)
- **EMET:** "abominable"
- **LOGOS:** "abominable"
- **PNEUMA:** "corrupt"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows EMET/LOGOS. The Greek *ebdelugmenois* ("having been made abominable") is specific; PNEUMA's "corrupt" is too general.

### Verse 8 (continued)
- **EMET:** "their part [is] in the lake burning with fire and sulfur"
- **LOGOS:** "their part shall be in the lake that burns with fire and sulphur"
- **PNEUMA:** "their destiny is the lake that blazes with fire and sulfur"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. PNEUMA's "destiny" interprets *meros* ("part/portion") and "blazes" intensifies the Greek participle.

### Verse 10
- **EMET:** "he carried me away in spirit upon a great and high mountain"
- **LOGOS:** "he carried me away in the Spirit to a great and high mountain"
- **PNEUMA:** "He carried me away in the Spirit to the summit of a vast, towering mountain"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. PNEUMA's "summit of a vast, towering mountain" dramatically expands the Greek. EMET's lowercase "spirit" and "upon" are less standard than LOGOS's "the Spirit" and "to."

### Verse 11
- **EMET:** "Her radiance [was] like a most precious stone, as a jasper stone, crystal-clear"
- **LOGOS:** "Her radiance was like a most precious stone, as a jasper stone, clear as crystal"
- **PNEUMA:** "The city shone with a radiance like the most precious gemstone -- like jasper, clear as crystal"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. PNEUMA adds "The city shone with" which is not in the Greek, and uses "gemstone" instead of "stone."

### Verse 12
- **EMET:** "Having a wall great and high, having twelve gates"
- **LOGOS:** "Having a great and high wall, having twelve gates"
- **PNEUMA:** "It had a massive, soaring wall with twelve gates"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS's word order. PNEUMA's "massive, soaring" is an interpretive expansion of "great and high."

### Verse 15
- **EMET:** "a golden measuring-reed"
- **LOGOS:** "a golden measuring rod"
- **PNEUMA:** "a gold measuring rod"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. The Greek *kalamon* can be "reed" or "rod"; "rod" is more natural in English for a measuring instrument. PNEUMA's "gold" (adjective) vs. "golden" is a minor difference; "golden" is preferred.

### Verse 17
- **EMET:** "one hundred forty-four cubits, [by the] measure of a man, which is [that] of an angel"
- **LOGOS:** "one hundred and forty-four cubits, the measure of a man, that is, of an angel"
- **PNEUMA:** "one hundred and forty-four cubits thick, by human measurement (which the angel was using)"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. PNEUMA adds "thick" (not in the Greek) and paraphrases the enigmatic parenthetical into an explanatory gloss.

### Verse 20
- **EMET:** "the sixth sardius" / "the eleventh hyacinth"
- **LOGOS:** "the sixth, sardius" / "the eleventh, jacinth"
- **PNEUMA:** "the sixth carnelian" / "the eleventh jacinth"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA adopts LOGOS's "sardius" (standard transliteration of *sardion*) over PNEUMA's "carnelian" (a modern mineral name). For the eleventh stone, SOPHIA adopts "jacinth" (LOGOS/PNEUMA) over EMET's "hyacinth." Both translate *huakinthos*, but "jacinth" is the standard gemological term.

### Verse 22
- **EMET:** "a temple I did not see in her" (preserving Greek word order)
- **LOGOS:** "I saw no temple in it"
- **PNEUMA:** "I saw no temple in the city"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. EMET's inverted word order preserves the Greek emphasis but is unnatural in English. PNEUMA's "in the city" makes explicit what "in it" already conveys.

### Verse 22 (continued)
- **EMET:** "her temple"
- **LOGOS:** "its temple"
- **PNEUMA:** "its temple"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS/PNEUMA. The feminine pronoun "her" (EMET) reflects the Greek feminine *autēs* (agreeing with *polis*, "city"), but English convention uses "its" for cities.

### Verse 23
- **EMET:** "illuminated her"
- **LOGOS:** "illumined it"
- **PNEUMA:** "is its light"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. The Greek *ephōtisen autēn* is aorist ("illumined"), which LOGOS preserves. PNEUMA shifts to present tense and paraphrases.

### Verse 24
- **EMET/LOGOS:** "the kings of the earth bring their glory into it"
- **PNEUMA:** "the kings of the earth will bring their splendor into it"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows EMET/LOGOS. PNEUMA's "splendor" replaces "glory" (*doxan*) and adds a future tense not present in the Greek present indicative *pherousin*.

### Verse 25
- **EMET:** "her gates will not be shut by day -- for night will not be there"
- **LOGOS:** "its gates shall never be shut by day, for there shall be no night there"
- **PNEUMA:** "Its gates will never be shut during the day -- and there will be no night there"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. Minor differences in phrasing; LOGOS's "shall never" is the most precise rendering of *ou mē kleisthōsin*.

### Verse 27
- **EMET:** "anything common, nor [anyone] doing abomination and falsehood, except those having been written in the book of life of the Lamb"
- **LOGOS:** "anything unclean, nor one who practices abomination and falsehood, but only those who are written in the Lamb's book of life"
- **PNEUMA:** "nothing impure will ever enter it, nor anyone who practices what is shameful or deceptive -- only those whose names are written in the Lamb's Book of Life"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. "Unclean" captures *koinon* in its Jewish purity-law sense. PNEUMA's "shameful or deceptive" reinterprets "abomination and falsehood," and PNEUMA again adds "names" and capitalizes "Book of Life."

--- SOPHIA
